question: How many total pieces of writing have I completed since I started writing again three weeks ago, including short stories, poems, and pieces for the writing challenge?

retrieval_results.context:
User is actively seeking ways to organize their writing projects, manage schedules and deadlines, and maintain creative flow, having recently written 17 poems in the past two weeks and feeling proud and creatively fulfilled.
The user plans to categorize their writing files by genre and project, creating folders for short stories, poetry, and writing challenge submissions, with subfolders for each prompt such as "Forgotten Memories" and "Future Prompts", and intends to use note-taking apps to brainstorm and outline stories.
User is considering using writing prompts or exercises to start each Pomodoro session before transitioning into the main writing project, aiming to warm up creative muscles and spark ideas.
The user is participating in a writing challenge on a social media platform, receiving a prompt each week; last week’s prompt was "forgotten memories", for which they wrote a piece titled "The Smell of Old Books".
The user will experiment with Evernote and Trello to find the best tool for their workflow, setting up dedicated notebooks and boards for each short story, poetry collection, and writing challenge prompt, using the web clipper for research and inspiration.
User plans to try Pomofocus and Focus Booster to determine which works best, and is evaluating whether Pomodoro timers are suitable for poetry writing sessions versus longer projects like short stories.
User is interested in exploring Pomodoro timers, specifically wants more information on how they work and how they can help maintain focus during writing sessions.
User wants to review progress and adjust the approach after each Pomodoro session, to track accomplishments, identify improvement areas, and remain flexible in the writing process.
The user seeks advice on how to brainstorm and develop characters, develop plot and structure, and revise and edit their stories.
User is interested in writing from the perspective of an inanimate object and wants tips on how to do so effectively
